reasoning: The question asks if exactly 10-12 SpaceX Starships will reach Space in 2025. This narrow band requires balancing both the possibility of achieving a higher cadence (13+ launches) and the substantial risk of delays or anomalies leading to fewer than 10 successful flights. Currently, SpaceX has achieved a cadence of about 4-6 launches per year. Even with ambitious plans to reach up to 25 or more, regulatory hurdles, technical complexities, pad refurbishments, and weather typically constrain realized rates. Given these factors, 10-12 successful launches represents a plausible but narrow scenario. Therefore, a modest probability is appropriate, reflecting the slim margin of exact numerical targets within a wide range of uncertainties.
